Also, towards the end of the semester, it seems that she gets lazy with her notes and does not explain any problems. She expects you to figure out how to do problems and does not work through them during lectures.

Dr. Cerkez is a really good Chemistry professor. I took her for Gen Chem 2. The course material is hard but she does a really good job to make us understand. She does a thing called "Murkiest Topic Poll" each week that allows you to say what you were confused about and she takes the time to explain it to you. One of the best chem professors so far.
Went in here to warn everyone, one of the worst profs I have encountered in my time at temple

Cerkez is a good Chemistry Professor. Lecture notes are organized and detailed so you don't have to read the book. She mainly goes over the concepts and expects you to use the homework as practice. You should have a solid understanding of math because she can go over the math problems quickly but with homework and extra practice you should be fine

Dr. Cerkez is the best chemistry professors at Temple. If you have a chance to take her, take her! She is super helpful during her office hours and will send you detailed e-mails before exams if you e-mail her questions. The textbook is helpful, but it is optional. She provides practice worksheets for each chapter. Her exams are hard, though.

Cerkez is awesome when it comes to tech labs. Compared to other lab TA, she is the best. Grades all the labs on time and gives good feedback so you don't make the same mistake on the next lab report. She is very approachable during office hours and answers all questions.
Dr Cerkez is a really nice professor! That being said, her teaching style can be a bit confusing at times, but if you pay attention and go to class, you will be fine.

Really good professor, but not worth it to take honors chem lab. Take regular chem lab with honors lecture instead. Lab reports are long and tedious. If you do the work, you can get an A, but the amount of work is not worth it for a 1 credit class.

Shes has a great personailty , she kinda depends on her TA's a lot though so if you TA sucks it might be rough , She does give pop quizzes like 4 that are 10 pts. She gives 20 questions for Sapling (homework) , you can use the openstax textbook to study from. she gives study guides and makes her own test. Still a hard class though

She'll definitely make you work for it, but Professor Cerkez is really good. She excepts you to be mature and take responsibility for this class but she is hilarious and easy to talk to. Graded by 10 out of 11 lab quizzes (make sure you write a lot in lab notebook!), 7 experiments, lab notebook, and final. Work hard and you'll do well.

Lab quizzes aren't that hard if you read the lab before hand. Make sure you show up on time. Tough grader on the lab reports, but leaves helpful comments. Very nice, funny. Will help you with the lab where she can. Always demonstrates new techniques before we begin.
very understanding of students but her lectures are so heavy and overwhelimg- don't think i would take again
She cares about the class and her students. Even though the material is difficult, she does her best to make sure everyone understands.

Fair tests, fair grading, and a great teacher. If you take good notes, do practice problems, and put time into the online homework, you will do well. Her notes are very clear and concise. She is happy to see you and is eager to help in office hours. 10/10 would take again. She is the best science professor I've had at temple thus far
She's a good lecturer but her tests are insane and in some cases very difficult to prepare for. If you're a chemistry wizard she's pretty great, but if you're not she's okay at best.

Took her for chem 1 and 2. Amazing lectures where she gives you amazing notes. One of best chem teachers at Temple. You need to put in a lot of work to keep up though. Review the notes and do textbook problems and you will do well. Always available to explain when you are confused. Does not curve though. Still tests are fair if you put in work.

I took her for both Chem 1031 and 1032. She's very organized and knows what she is doing. Do come to lectures because she does give out pop-quizzes. Her lecture is very easy to follow. 4 exams - drop the lowest one. Her exams is very fair.
Excellent teacher. Explains all material very well. Very clear about what is needed to know for testing. Fair grader, drops some low grades.

Dr. Cerkez was such an easy professor to understand. She is very clear about lecture material, grades, what will be on the exams, and more. She is very approachable so don't be scared to go to her if you have questions. As long as you show up to class and actually review the material you'll definitely pass!

I had her for Gen Chem 1 and 2. She is awesome. You'll have to work in chem as it is not easy, but I studied outside of class and did all the homework and finished with a B+. If you work hard you'll do well and she is very willing to help you out as long as youre doing your part. She's very fair and good at teaching material during lecture

Very good teacher, she writes the notes on the board so you don't have to stress thinking about what notes to take. no electronics allowed in her class so you have to handwrite notes but its not too bad. Moderate exams and the curve is pretty good. Had some pop quizzes (like 3?) for attendance purposes. Go to her office hours if you have questions!

I took my first semester Chemistry class with Dr. Cerkez. First of all, the lecture hall is 250 + people and she writes the notes on the board each and every class. It's annoying, and it's hard to see since there is no way everyone can sit up close. So if you miss one lecture, you can't get the notes unless you know someone.

She's a very good lecturer. Go to class, because she gives a few pop quizzes to check for attendance. The tests are harder than the material learned during the lecture, so make sure to read the textbook. She cares about her students & answers emails in a timely fashion.

Solid choice for Chem 1. Her lectures are straightforward and she's very easy to understand. The exams are lecture-heavy so it's a good idea to pay attention in class, she prepares you well. Some pop quizzes here and there. She's a very organized professor and one of your best choices compared to some of the other (harder) chem professors.

Cerkez is a very good professor who gives clear notes and is very approachable. She also tends to give out a few pop lecture quizzes, so make sure you go to class. Although she's a great professor, her tests are lengthy and harder than the material that is gone over in class, so be sure to go over textbook problems.

Professor Cerkez is a very difficult professor. She clearly knows what she is talking about and will explain any material to you if you don't understand. The only bad part about taking her section is that she her test are very intensive (difficult) and also lengthy compared to the other professors. Overall her course is very difficult.

Professor Cerkez is a good teacher. She is very straightforward and approachable. If you pay attention in class and have all the notes, you will easily pass her tests. There are some pop-quizzes so go to class.

The lab for this class was definitely difficult and every lab took me upwards of 3-4 hours to complete, and Cerkez is a hard grader, but she clearly cares about what she is doing and will explain everything thoroughly and answer all your questions. It was a very work heavy lab but I learned a lot

I feel like this class if very transparent in what she wants. This class is definitely easier with Cerkez teaching. She doesn't waste time. Very straight forward and to the point. Go to class because she will have pop quizzes to check on attendance. She is very approachable and easy to go to to ask questions.

I LOVE DR. GILBERT! He is an amazing professor, if you ever need him to open an assignment just ask! He does not like it when your talking or not paying attention in lecture. He does sometimes cold all people in the back, but it usually pretty funny. As long as you do the practice midterms you should be fine

Professor Gilbert is great! He is on top of the work, the assignments help in learning, and quizzes and tests directly reflect what you are learning. He even adds some extra credit questions and has grading policies that benefit the students.

One of the best professors I've had in the science department. He truly cares about students passing and offers plenty of extra credit opportunities. Sometimes curves the grades, which really helps. His lectures can be fun and engaging. I would definitely recommend taking his class!

LOVE PROF GILBERT!! I struggled in chem (just not my strong suit) but he is still an amazing teacher. Very engaging, caring, and accommodating. If you are not strong in chem or math PLEASE take his course. He is willing to give extra credit and provide help. Attendance isn't mandatory but is counted and must be above 80% to get an A for partic.

honestly such a good guy. I was not going to pass after the final exam and he gave me what I needed to pass because I had a perfect attendance score in lecture, is so understanding and truthfully one of the nicest professors ever. He really cares about your success. As for classes he has great and engaging lectures. take Dr Gilbert for any gen chem

Very good professor. He was funny, came to lecture prepared, and clearly explained things. All of his tests are multiple choice, but if you come to class (he does take attendance that's worth 100 points later, so I advise you do) and do the practice exams, you'll do great. Just try not to pull your phone out in front of him.

Yikes. He is funny, but I HATE his teaching style. I barely learned anything, had to teach it to myself with the homework videos. Don't use phones in lecture, I hear him call out kids for that a lot. Hard to do well, but I hear all chem professors here are like this or worse. Attendance is 100 points so do it. 4 mid terms, 1 final, 10 quizzes.

Dr. Gilbert made lectures informative and enjoyable. He came prepared each lectures. He performed multiple demos throughout the course. All the notes were posted on Canvas for review and as a study guide. Practice exams were posted for review as well. The exams were all multiples choice; they were graded and posted the same day.

Really enjoyed his class, made the lectures funny and genuinely intriguing. It's clear he is very passionate about quantum chemistry, inorganics, and often describes different Nobel Prize winners to make the knowledge learned in the class applicable. Best in the chem department, hands down.
Out of all the Chem 1032

Lowkey terrible teacher. I think his rating is high because everyone else that teaches Chem II at Temple is truly way worse. Best option to take, but it's hard to pass. He also has a habit of calling out and being rude to random student throughout lecture which is super uncomfortable. Glad to be out of his reach!

Nice and funny dude but i did not really learn chem in the class. Since it was chem4engineers he made it a lot easier than it should be, no homework (which is good but you get no self practice). 4 midterms with lowest one dropped, and 6 quizzes with 2 dropped. I honestly only understood the formulas which was enough to get by.

Funny guy but lowkey terrible at teaching this class. He goes through worksheets in class (these questions show up on exams) but doesn't explain at all what he's doing or why, he just goes through the problems as if you already know the entire topic. I spent so much time outside of class watching videos and teaching myself the content. Hes cool tho

Look no further. You'll actually want to go to all his lectures. He's interesting, funny, relaxed, and one hell of a scientist. You'll leave with so much practical real-world chemistry knowledge that he seamlessly bakes into his lectures that you'll want to take him again.

By far one of my favorite professors at Temple. His style of teaching is very clear and he makes going to class something to look forward to. I learned a great deal of information in comparison to other courses I've taken. He loves to tell random, somewhat related stories too while teaching.
Really nice guy. Makes lectures less boring, always has something interesting to say.

Did I learn a lot about chemistry? Not really (took AP in highschool). Did I have an absolutely blast? YES! Love this dude, absolutely hilarious. Easy quizzes and tests. If you are a ME or ECE and don't need real chem take chem for engr with this dude (DON'T take normal chem you will regret it so much). This class is a joke how easy it is.
Very Horrible, won't take him again, does not care for his students, Does not answer your questions on time have to email him twice, Does not want his students to get succeed.
Horrible lectures. Doesn't take his job seriously and he doesn't care about his students progress. He can be very rude and impatient. Never taking his lecture again. Worst class of my life
Content is slightly challenging but still very easily doable especially with his and the TA's handouts. Hilarious guy and makes an 8 am chemistry class something to look forward to.

Lectures can be boring (it's chemistry, come on), but he makes the class less bleak. I actually find myself looking forward to his classes sometimes to wash away the dread I feel after some of my other lectures. Easy content, easy tests, easy class. Doesn't take attendance and posts exam dates so you can just study and show up for exams, easy A.

As someone who hates learning chemistry topics, professor Gilbert made them very entertaining and simple. Class is short and easy and the exams aren't very difficult. He's funny and down to earth which makes class enjoyable.
He tells a lot of jokes, and has entertaining lectures
Great professor, easy course. he cracks a lot of jokes and makes the classroom a fun place to be. I had him in Fall of 2019.

Adv Inorganic was a good class with Dr. Gilbert. You spend a lot of time on group theory but if you've taken PChem 2, it is actually quite insightful and explains a lot of things we assume. Exams are straightforward if you read the notes; there aren't any surprises. Just make sure to read. Cool class, good lecture, only problem is the 8 am start.

It's not Prof Gilbert's fault, but whoever created the set up of this online general chemistry class doesn't deserve a job. The class was set up so that you don't have to go to lectures or any zooms, you only have to complete the readings and quizzes for each module. I genuinely feel like I learned nothing in this class.

Very helpful as he isnt my assigned professor and took the leadership and helped me during office hours with a reply back within 5-10 minutes (as I was unsure out of the 3 professors which I was supposed to contact). Otherwise, the online chem lecture / structure was an absolute nightmare for students and this prof made it a little easier.

His zoom lectures help you complete the modules. He goes over the questions on the module quizzes with different numbers. He is very funny and gets to know his students (if you show up). Would take him again, but I didn't like how CHEM 1031 was set up this online semester in general.

I love Dr. Gilbert. He can be a little hard to understand sometimes but it's not too bad. He makes funny comments a lot in class, he is an excellent professor, and he gives a lot of practice worksheets and practice midterms which are helpful for the exams. I got sick during the semester and he was extremely caring and accommodating.

Very friendly and enjoyable lecture. He always answered questions and was not out to give anyone a hard time. My only complaint is if you like lots of practice problems you will not have many. He usually only provided one example problem Per chapter and its from the book. So you wont get much variation on harder problems to practice.

Had Dr. Gilbert for Crystallography. He was honestly really helpful and really wanted us to know the content rather than giving more difficult questions. Definitely learned a lot, and he is super approachable and just has a great personality. The course itself taught me a lot of cool stuff and I highly recommend. If he's an option, do it.
If you go to his office hours and speak with him about your grade he will definitely help you out. Also he curves the exams which helps everyone. Best professor for gen chem 2!

There's barely any online hw, the recitation quizzes are pretty difficult but can boost your score if you do well in them, his tests are hard as well. But he's a good teacher and funny too. I suggest you take him, he also curves nicely

Very thorough lectures, and keeps the class attentive despite the weariness of waking up at 8am. Great sense of humor too, and also open to help students in and out of class. Not necessarily from the prof, but make sure to keep up with lectures as pop quizzes (recitation) will drop your grade fast if you don't pay attention well.
No curve
He was easily the most hilarious chem prof. His lectures were a hit or miss but if you show up the test should be easy. he would always make the class enjoyable with his whisky jokes. cool dude

Dr. Gilbert is one of the funniest professors that I've had at Temple. He really gets to know everyone (provided that you actually show up) and tells a lot of interesting stories. My only complaint of the class is that he glosses over a lot of topics and I personally would have enjoyed the class more if we went more in depth.

Even though the material may be challenging, Dr. Gilbert does a really good job of conveying it in a way that most people can understand it. He also uses a lot of examples to help you prepare. Basically, if you go to class and pay some attention, read the book, and you'll be fine.
Go to class, take notes, read the powerpoint slides in prep for tests. You will do fine. Great teacher

Great guy. Took him for Chemistry for Engineers as I think he's the only professor that teaches the course. Really funny, passionate about his subject, and very sensible grader. Don't play on your phone or computer during class, though, you will get called out!

Pros -easy midterms and final -no quizzes, projects or assignments -barely any homework -lecture slides uploaded on Blackboard -no book required, slides are enough Cons -his accent might seem a bit thick at first -

Gilbert can't teach. His lectures are confusing, he doesn't follow the syllabus, his exams have many typos that need to be corrected DURING THE EXAM. I learned from his online lecture notes that he never uses. Anyway, I'd say avoid, but he's the only one that teaches chem for engineers.
If you enjoy chemistry and have a good grasp on the basics before taking the class, it is very enjoyable. Sit near the front so you can hear/understand his lectures better!

Professor Gilbert was the best! I would not say his exams are easy but if you read the textbook, do the worksheets he posts, and put in the effort, you are sure to get an A. I took this class during a rough time in my life and gilbert really made me lighten up especially with his life advice rampages in lecture. Overall, I would 100% recommend.

Was a tough grader, but went off topic a lot in lecture (which is a good thing) and joked around and what not. He was all around a good teacher and a good person. Very easy to talk to and helped out A LOT in office hours! Use them and you'll pass, no doubt!!
Lecture was okay. Some days he put everyone to sleep, other days were really fun. He tries to make test easier by giving you formulas but the tests are still hard.

He is a very good chemistry teacher and is a funny guy. I rarely went to class and did fine in this course. Don't let him catch you on your phone in class or he will roast you in front of everyone. Definitely would take him again. Didn't use the textbook at all because he posts all the notes online, but it may be helpful if you're not great at chem

Hilarious lecture! Don't skip though, its easy to get lost in the class. Overall great at explaining things and willing to spend time during office hours to go over any questions. Tests aren't that hard he gives practice exams a few days before the actual test and its pretty similar. Overall a great professor!
Hr is such an amazing teacher and I would definitely take him again.

Very good at explaining concepts. The language barrier can be tough at first, but it's not a problem once you get use to it. He is very friendly and eager to help during office hours. ZERO TOLERANCE for cheating, He WILL kick you out of an exam if he catches you. Keeps lectures interesting, I would absolutely take him again.

TAKE GILBERT. Huge curve and hilarious. DO NOT CHEAT. He will call you out and rip up your exam. One student said " I swear to God I wasn't cheating" and Gilbert replied "Too bad I don't believe in God." Does not want to have you again so he will pass you with a C even if you have a 60. Gives 4 exams and your lowest is dropped. Final was hard tho
He's a nice guy and very funny, but very paranoid about people cheating. Chem is a tough class so you need to put in work but he has a really big curve so you should be fine.

This man was horrible at teaching. We thought our selves the entire course basically. Tests were brutally hard, average was a 65. His final average was a 58. He tells you, he passes a 60 which is a complete lie. He doesn't care about any students and we all are cheaters unless proven otherwise. If you want to succeed in chemistry take someone else.

Great teacher, really funny and helpful. He understands that students have classes and lives outside of his class and is sure to cater to that. Tests are easy, final is pretty difficult but if you prepare well you'll be fine!
Quirky and timely sense of humor, efficient lecturer and problem solver. Homework is infrequent and not difficult.
Best Chem professor at temple. One of the few good professor and a really nice guy. Not many personable people but Gilbert is 1.

Great professor. He has a thick accent but is hilarious once you learn how to understand it. Attendance is definetely optional as he spends about half the class periods telling funny stories. As long as you get ready for each exam by doing the practice problems he posts you will do fine. Id take another class with him.
Attend lectures and you should do fine.
Good prof. but you need to work too

Gilbert is a nice man, with a heavy accent. His 4 midterms aren't hard, but the final is. Attendance is not mandatory. Buying the textbook is important. There's online homework that isn't impossible, but still not easy. I would recommend taking him, it's just a difficult subject.

Gilbert is a nice man & his tests are pretty easy. However,I found myself consistently teaching myself the entire course. I did well on the 4 midterms (he drops your lowest one), however I failed the final (the average was failing). It dropped my grade from an A to a B. So be weary that his class is easier than the other chem & it hinders learning.

I had to teach myself from the book. He gets off topic, skips power points, and here is a language barrier. Just because he is easy does not mean you will pass the class. If you're good at self teaching, take him, otherwise take a prof that offers more of a challenge.

Dr. Gilbert one of the best in chemistry teaching. He let the course go through so easily. His exams are pretty much easy to handle. Also his power-points is a good source to study from as it summarizes the textbook very well. Not as many says, he prepare you well for the final. All in all, Dr. Gilbert is HIGHLY RECOMMENDED for an easy A.

Dr. Gilbert is a great professor who at times may seem easy, but he does a great job of teaching the material. His exams are extremely manageable and you should get at least an 84. Use his powerpoints since some of the questions come straight from the powerpoint. He is also very kind and helpful during office hours. Don't forget to use the textbook

Gilbert is the complete package- he is a great guy, a great professor, and his exams are totally manageable. He can move a little fast during lectures, but his powerpoints are very clear and easy to understand. He is one of my favorite professors I've had a Temple. Highly recommend.
Gets off topic SO OFTEN. Do not recommend if your major is chemistry dependent or if you actually want to learn in class.
He relates class content to real life pretty well, but he gets off topic fairly often.

Chem is chem. Its gonna be a little harder for most to grasp. Gilbert is awesome! Relates material to everyday life making it a lot easier to retain and understand ... also has an awesome sense of humor!! Highly recommended.

Material comes out of textbook so just study that with the powerpoints. Only showed up on test days and office hours and got an A. Very helpful in office hours and overall a pretty cool guy. Explains things well and calls on people in class.

Good Teacher, his lectures are straight to the point and easy to understand, though he does have an accent. I thought his test were pretty easy but the class is graded on a curve, so you are graded on how well you did prepared to others. I believe that he did a very good job of preparing us for the final and for gen chem 2. Also pretty funny.
this was hard, chemistry is hard but he does curve. His exams are powerpoint based so definitely study that. he is a pretty funny professor also.
Tests are definitely easier than price or childs study the practice test he posts on line and you will get at least a B on every test. He is also teaches well and has a good sense of humor.

I will give you true to the honest review :-His tests are different from the practice tests. Rely heavily on Textbook to get above 84+. His grades are curve so its hard to get below C.He is a nice guy and will answer your questions, if you ask him. He is one of the best Chem professor and if you study an hour or 2 a week you will get an A.
Great guy, funny/quirky sense of humor that is greatly appreciated. Follows textbook closely, but its a good thing. Grading policy is fair, midterms pretty accurately reflect what is taught in class.
Funny teacher, but not helpful. His midterms were real easy and then the final was super hard because he doesn't prepare you. Don't email him a question because he won't get back to you.
easy tests, very helpful, boring lectures. he kinda picks on people during class and call you out on things but other than that he's pretty nice and funny. i'd take him again

A very funny teacher, sometimes the accent prevents you from understanding his lectures. But everything that you need to know for the midterms is on his powerpoints. Even though his midterms might be easy and straight from the powerpoints, does not really prepare you for the final. Overall a very nice and helpful teacher.

He's awesome. He has a good sense of humor. His test are easier then the rest of the chemistry classes. He does powerpoints but they are very helpful. If you study the notes and powerpoints you will do great on the midterms

He's a very fun professor, very witty. He's pretty thorough in what he teachers, but during lectures, he'll skip between the powerpoint and handwritten notes pretty quickly, which can seem a bit disorganized, but so is the mind of a science person :P Anyway, the exams are okay, but he does offer review sessions and office hours- use them!
Very helpful and very nice. Tests are straight from the powerpoints.

Im in the business school, and took chem as an elective. he is so much more laidback than most professors, and is pretty cool to students who are respectful. I mostly took this class bc of the easy ratings he had on here. Class required a little more work than I thought, and was actually somewhat challenging at times, but I still managed an A.

I took this course summer 2007. Prof.Gilbert is very helpful and uses funny powerpoint slides to make orgo. interesting. He gives out a lot of bonus points for attending class,and really wants his students to succeed. Best science prof. I've had.

Great guy. Tries to be funny and most times, he succeeds. Quizzes every week and several homework assignments but relatively easy. Exams are almost straight from his lecture notes (PowerPoint slides,) which are provided to students in advance of class.

Gilbert is great! Definitely take his class. He makes great slide shows that tell you exactly what to know for the tests and he is a really funny guy. I had him for technically a one day a week 4 hour night class and he always gave us at least a 15min break and sometimes 5pts extra credit on exams if you stayed for the 2nd half. Seriously, take him

Great teacher with lots of patients. His powerpoint slides are helpful and hes great for office hours. Pretty funny and always picked people out in class for cell phone use and sleeping. He knows the material and can present it clearly. Tests are good if you just do your work.
Great teacher!! The best chem teacher I ever had. Makes no assumptions that you know your stuff. Highly recommend him.

He is truly the best teacher I ever had. I dont care what any other ratings say, this man is amazing. He curves all the test so that everyone have a chance to pass. His tests are super EASY. what more can you ask for? The man care about his students and want everyone to pass. I wish I could take him for all my chemistry classes.

This guy is AWESOME!! The best Chemistry teacher ever, turns what may be a dull subject into something interesting. He is funny as hell too. His tests are fair if you study the material and attend class.
Great teacher! Best chem teacher! Tests are hard but the curves are amazing!

Dr. Gilbert can be hard to understand sometimes. But if you have a question, he will never make you feel dumb. He is humorous, a fair grader, a nice guy. Knowledgable, interesting. If you have to take Chemistry, try to get him for a teacher.
hes not easy; hard to understand if u dont read or pay attn; sleepers beware, he will point u out! haha ... he's funny though

Dr. Gilbert is very intelligent. He is nice and fun to have as your professor. His test is easy as long you are prepared for his class. I have him for Inorganic Synthesis which is a lab course and he made the lab class such a fun experience. He is willing to help you if you have any questions. An excellant professor.

This man is awesome.... he is by far one of the best prof. I have ever had. He is all about helping you. From day one he had the mindframe that everyone can get an A in organic and he will do anything he can to help you. I don't think he is staying at Temple but if he is still here and you need to take the class make sure it is with him.
No Comments
He's a good teacher. He has a indian accent.. but you can live with it.. he also tries to be funny.. tests arent soo bad

He shows a genuine care for your ability to learn and understand the material. It's obvious that Dr. Han is enamored with Pchem and his enthusiasm seeps into every lab and lecture. His grading is tough but honest and fair, and for god's sake, don't cheat. He's always willing to help, even out of class. You get as much as you give with him!

Pchem lab 1 was fast paced but very fair. Dr. Han tried his best to cover as much material as possible while focusing on our understanding. His love for the course truly shines through in his lectures and lab time. He's a very enthusiastic chemist and teacher and his love and respect for chemistry is seen during every meeting. Highly recommend!

If you're looking to take PChem 1 lecture and Dr. Han is teaching the course, you need to take it that semester. Dr. Han does a fantastic job at teaching the PChem courses, and you will succeed with him. The courses (1 and 2) have quizzes and homework, but he grades them fairly and they are great for preparing for exams.

PChem 2 Lab contains less labs than 1's Lab, with 2 full reports and 2 brief reports. The subject matter also is more difficult, but luckily Dr. Han is in charge of the course. Dr. Han makes the course fun and gives you dedicated classes to data analysis with him and the TA. Dr. Han cares about his students and his passion for teaching is obvious.

PChem 1 Lab contains quite a few labs and reports, but the class is not writing intensive, so the reports are relatively short. Dr. Han is a fair grader and always helps if you run into issues during the lab or data analysis. Also, Dr. Han encourages participation during lab lectures but never looks down on people who give wrong answers.

This class is definitely hard work, but Dr. Han makes it fun! He offers a lot of help outside of class, and that helps a lot! The experiments are really interesting and that makes the class even for fun!

Dr. Han makes this writing intensive course materials easy to understand although difficult. His lecture notes are vey helpful and the relevant chapters of the textbook are posted on canvas. Very accessible outside class and helps a lot during office hours

Chem3397 is of course intensive writing course in Physical Chemistry, and having a professor like Dr. Han makes it less stressful. Textbooks are not required since he posts the relevant aspects of the textbooks on canvas for each experiment. His lecture notes are very helpful and is there to help when you need him. Definitely a great prof.

Dr. Han is one of the best professors in the chemistry department. He is always accessible and his classes are straight forward with clear instruction. He makes difficult pchem concepts easy to understand and gives great feedback on lab reports. You have to put in the work but will learn a lot from Dr. Han.

PCHEM lab was definitely difficult course but Dr. Han understood all the situation and was really caring person. I can not say that the lectures were easy but with an instructor like Dr. Han, you will be able to pass the course. Highly recommended!

Pchem is definitely difficult and by no means an easy A, so it absolutely helps to have an instructor like Dr. Han that understands this and is very willing to help and work with you - he wants his students to understand and succeed. His lectures are clear and well-paced. He puts all notes & lectures online and organizes it so it's easy to locate

Dr. Han uses every bit of class time. If you think you will get out of lab early, you won't. People say he is nice and at first he will seem so, but he can also be extremely condescending. If you ask for help, he will talk over your head and waste your time. The only positive to his lab are the workshops where you dissect parts of your lab.

Dr. Han is a gem of the chemistry department. He is always willing to address your problems and give your direct, explicit answers which help you in writing your lab report. He is cognizant of the stressors that an online chemistry course can entail, and hence is willing to work with you about anything. Very non-judgmental, and always happy to help

IF you took pchem 1 class format is the same in every way except the four topics. Dr Han is one of my favorite teachers ive had at temple. Does not waste anyones time, lectures are detailed and always relevant, whenever I had a problem or question I always left class or lab with my issue resolved.

Professor Han is one of the best professors I have ever had in college. He teaches with passion and moves at a speed everyone can follow. Unlike most professors, he understands the demands of a STEM major and he skillfully schedules due dates giving students a reasonable amount of time to complete each assignment.

Jun han is a great professor. He has always helped me with any confusions. Pchem courses are extremely difficult but Dr. Han actually made sure to reach out with helping hands. Its was him who made this subject seem fun. He always try to crack a joke in the class to break the tension. As it is this subject had very serious environment.

This was definitely not an easy class, however, Dr. Han breaks up the topic and taught well and clearly. He places a lot of emphasis on what he expects and looks for in the paper. Labs are fun but the papers were not easy to write. I recommend going to him if you have any questions since he is very helpful and kind. Class is also very organized.

An extremely humble Professor, yet so intelligent. Very helpful; great with office hours and meeting with students. His lectures are very informative and he promotes an easy environment to learn. One of, if not the best Professor I've had at Temple University.

Dr. Han deserves the world. He is probably the nicest man in the chemistry department and really wants you to learn and have fun, despite expecting A LOT. Wish they let him teach actual lectures; he is honestly one of the smartest people out there and his teaching skills are god level. If he is the TA, prof, whatever, take him.
Dr. Han is a very nice professor, his course material is very useful. He is very patient to solve your question on his class.
He is very rude and demeaning to any of his students that ask questions.

Dr.Jasmin is a great prof that cares about his students. While his lectures can be all over the place and hard to understand, they teach you everything you need to know for the exams. Very helpful for office hours as well.

Orgo isn't an easy class but Jasmin wants students to succeed. His exams are tough but he curves them a lot. I considered dropping but after talking with him he encouraged me to stay and said passing was possible. There is ALEKS homework and recitation quizzes outside class, and recitation attendance matters. Stay consistent and try your best.

Awful, just don't. Organic chem is tough but If you wish to learn the material don't take him. If you want to pass or get a better grade by all means go ahead. I had better luck learning from videos online.

This class was hard and time consuming. He is very sarcastic, his humor might make you feel stupid but don't take it personal. You'll have to teach yourself but he does pay attention to who goes to lecture. I passed the class bc of the curve I had a 64% after my final grade and I passed w a C+. I will be taking him again just bc he passed me

If you want to learn orgo, do not take Jasmin. He mumbles through every lecture, and office hours include him screaming at students and making people uncomfortable. The curve will save you, but you definitely do not see the effort you put into the class in your grades. Like many other classes, teach yourself everything and do practice problems.

Despite the ratings, Dr. Jasmin isn't bad. He remembers who attends his lectures and office hours. Yes, the slides aren't the best way of learning, but he does this to subtly encourage learning through practice problems (ALEKS, Textbook, Bubble Sheet). Showing up matters. On an off day you might consider skipping lecture, he may offer extra credit.

Dr. Jasmin is hard to understand but the curve will save you. Attendance is not mandatory and does not make a difference but he does offer extra credit after exams to those who attend his lecture. take the class if you wanna pass orgo but wont really learn anything

Dr. Jasmin is alright. He does not have the greatest teaching style since he reads off a Powerpoint which is very unhelpful in organic chem. His lectures are extremely fast paced, but he does take questions. He is very helpful in office hours, but his exams are awfully hard. He curves a good amount, and there is a curve at the end of the semester.

Dr. Jasmin was an interesting professor. I did not love his lecture style of using PowerPoints to teach Organic Chemistry II, but the fact is that the class is heavily curved at the end of the term due to the difficulty of the exams compared to what he actually teaches in lecture. He greatly appreciates when people participate in his class.

Only passed because of his huge curves. Rude towards students who have questions, and he has his favorites. Office hours was a HUGE waste of time. The average for the second exam last semester was a 16% and we were not aware of the format change that might have helped us prepare better. Disorganized slides and always mumbles. 0/10 do NOT recommend!

I liked my time with him. people would disagree with me, he was much better than my Orgo 1 professor. I will say if I was someone debating taking his class TAKE HIM it's worth it. He is very sarcastic but he means well and if you go see him he is also very helpful. He does tend to scare a lot of students but at the end of the day he is the best.

He is both funny and scary, he knows when to crack out a joke, but will go down on you if you don't know something. A lot of students don't participate because he makes us feel stupid when we ask a question or even answer at that. But, as long as you keep to yourself, and form a study group you should do fine if you know the minimum content.

I have him for both lecture and recitation, he is extremely confrontational. He often asks questions and acts like the entire class is dumb when we have trouble answering. He hates when people don't pay attention but is also a horrible lecturer, I have better luck just reading from the textbook (also he semi-slurs when he talks)
I liked him, there's a big curve at the end anyways. He usually uses back exams, but changed it this semester.

Jasmin gets a lot of undeserved hate. He has dry humor that can be misinterpreted as attitude, but I think he is hilarious. His lectures come directly from the book, which I prefer to use over his lectures. He pays attention to who shows up to class will help your grade out if you show you care. Very smart, but doesn't dumb down difficult concepts.

I struggled very hard with Jasmin. Almost everyone in the class is failing and his grade is apparently what saves everyone. There are no review sessions, and just takes pictures out of the textbook to discuss at lecture. Way worse than my orgo 1 prof

Worst professor at Temple! Went to office hours multiple times and he was rude and sarcastic every time. Orgo is a hard subject and he doesn't prepare you well for it. You will have to teach yourself! The lectures are screenshots of the textbook. He provides practice exams that don't even resemble the real exams, test averages are in the 40s-50s.

Take any other orgo professor if possible please. I'm sure this man has had so many complaints to the department and I yet to know why he's still teaching. The class literally survives on his curve because his teaching is horrible. Random quiz grades from hw, and impossible to predict exams. You may study harder than ever, and it won't be worth it.

Personally, I think Jasmin gets much more hate than is warranted. Orgo 2 is tough but if you do the reading before class and actually study the material, its not that bad. Jasmin is a super nice guy and wants to see everyone succeed. He notices who pays attention and who goes to lecture so you're better off going.

Average professor- gave NO partial credit and was hard to understand. Orgo 2 is a hard class and you need to do work outside of class, but class averages were in the 30's/40's when people were studying hard. Pro's are that he has lots of office hours and the power points are helpful. Left class feeling like I learned nothing abt orgo 2.

Dr. Jasmin gets a lot of unfair criticism. Organic chemistry is difficult, but he does as much as he can to help his students out. He notices who goes to class and actively participates, and he will help those students out. He is also a trickster in terms of quizzes and tests, but he is always fair. Responds to emails quickly and gives a nice curve

Very difficult to understand, rude to students who asked questions or for clarification. Doesn't help make orgo any easier. Some days he's really nice and others he's extremely rude. Would skip through lecture slides when people did not answer his questions even though no one understood what he's even saying half the time. Only plus is his curving

His lectures contain a lot of material, all of which you need to memorize in order to do well on the exams. However, he is very willing to meet during office hours to help, and the class is graded on a bunch of different things, rather than just exams like most STEM courses, which helps a lot. He also curves well, and offers extra credit sometimes
It's a lot of information to pack into lectures, but he's great during recitation and helpful during office hours. Would take again.

I had heard a lot about Professor Jasmin before taking orgo, but he was definitely far better than his ratings. The class is challenging but I don't believe that is Jasmin's fault. He is difficult to understand and does go extremely fast, but taking the time outside of class to look over and actually do the slides helps so much.

Dr. Jasmin is a fantastic teacher. He's an expert in Organic Chemistry and very excited and passionate about the material. He is exceptional in office hours (really funny too!). This class needs self-study; lectures and homework sets will not be enough for you to develop sufficient fluency. Exams were difficult but doable (the curves will help too)

Probably the weirdest professor I've ever had, he is so unpredictable. Lectures are useless, I never read the book but thats probably a good idea. He curves very well, but be prepared to be on edge all semester because you won't know anything and you'll be banking on the curve. He is very nice and funny, go to recitation and participate!!!
Such a kind man and very educated in what he teaches. Will help you in office hours. Genuinely has your best interest in mind. Wish I took him for Orgo 1 too
Amazing, despite what you may or may not hear, he is THE BEST

Honestly, Jasmin is a great professor. He is accessible outside of class and is eager to teach students who actually want to learn. Although attendance is not mandatory he knows who goes to class and who does not. Try to outline the textbook and do every practice problem- orgo is a very difficult class so you have to put in work.

Lectures are useless; he reads from a PowerPoint, goes too fast, and is very difficult to understand. He also berates students who ask questions/are confused. All exam averages this semester were in the 20%s. He gave a pop quiz on the last day of recitation. Yes, he curves exams and final grades but is overall awful. Don't risk your mental health.

He lectures really fast. But I love this professor. He is so funny and a nice man. He always has office hours willing to help you. You can ask him any question and he is prepared to explain the entire concept to you, even drawing out mechanisms and details. Anyone taking Orgo for medical school should know its a hard class and you need to study.

What everyone else says about this class is about right. If you actually talk to Jasmin he is a very nice man but he also is a genius when it comes to orgo. He explains things in a way that is over everyone's heads unless you're amazing at orgo. The tests are hard but the curve is very generous though so he definitely compensates for that.

You won't understand anything he says during class. Take good notes from the textbook prior to class and it will help you understand better. If you read the textbook and do practice problems you will be fine.

If you want a good grade take Dr. Jasmin. All of his exams were curved and on top of that, there is a generous curve at the end of class. Back exams can be helpful, but don't completely rely on them (I made that mistake!!). Overall, Orgo's a tough class and Jasmin is a good professor to take.

Pretty horrible Professor, cant understand him and he just reads off of a PowerPoint. Likes to get snippy with students who ask a question, and most students fail his tests. However, the curve is insane at the end as you all know. Went from a 49 to a 71. Wanna pass, take Jasmin. Wanna learn Orgo? Take someone else.

Jasmin is probably the best orgo prof at temple. His exams are all curved and the grading scale is curved too. It's clear he wants you to get a good grade in the class and is very willing to help. He's a good lecturer, makes everything very clear and easy to understand. Orgo is hard, but he's a good professor overall.

His class is doable and Jasmin is the best option to take out of all the other instructors teaching Organic Chemistry. I would recommend students to take him because his exams are multiple choice along with some open ended questions. And the curve for this class is a blessing!

He makes Ochem harder. His teaching style sucks. Goes over PowerPoints for lecture. Homework are useless. Few quizzes and three tests. Syllabus unorganized. Never follows correct dates on the syllabus. If you just want an A take him because of his curve at the end of the semester. But If you want to actually learn about Ochem take someone else.

Let me start by saying this: organic chemistry is hard. I will most likely be failing this semester, but it was not Dr. Jasmin's fault. Dr. Jasmin is so kind and always there when you need help. I met with him on many occasions and always tried to explain the best that he could. I am just not good at ochem

The exams are all multiple choice, and they are the hardest exams in comparison to the other orgo professors. That being said, expect a fat curve at the end 10% for F2020. If you want to learn, take another professor. if you want to get a good grade in orgo, take jasmin!

Jasmin gets a lot of hate, but he is a solid choice for Orgo. The class is not easy and his lectures are all over the place, but he is hilarious in office hours and is always available to help. Exams are hard but doing a little better than the average (just 5%) you'll be fine. The curve at the end of the semester will save your grade.

He's a solid choice for orgo, and undoubtedly cares for those who are interested in learning. He is readily available to help, but he definitely knows who has been attending his classes (even if attendance isn't mandatory). The curve definitely helps out in the end. He was a great orgo teacher online, during the transition to Zoom.

The only reason why I would say to take him is the curve. He is rude and mean. And his exams are way easier compared to the other professors. However, I really think he has anger issues. If you get close to him though, I heard he warms up. CURVE CURVE CURVE though.

Jasmine gets a lot of unnecessary hate in my opinion. It's organic chemistry and it's not easy. You have to go to lecture and actually study for the exams. He's always available in office hours and as long as you perform above average ( even just 5% above ) on all the exams and actually study for the final, you will be fine. My 72% got curved to A-

He is hard to understand and gives difficult multiple choice/open ended exams that most people get 50s on. He also fell behind on material. However, all the talk about his curve is true and will raise your grade by like 20%. Do better than the average and you will pass comfortably but it's still orgo, so it definitely isn't a cakewalk.

If you take Jasmin for Orgo 1, you will not learn a thing. His class is very disorganized, the syllabus dates for exams are not correct so you can never plan ahead. His due dates are random and he's very hard to understand in lecture. Dont get me wrong, he seems like nice guy who wants to see you succeed but everything about this class sucks. Avoid
Worst professor I've ever taken. Please, spare yourself from this course. I didn't learn one thing and he uses the same exams each semester so everyone cheats. Not about that type of laziness.
Orgo is hard enough as it is. Do yourself a favor and just take Flemming if you want to understand anything at all.

Serge Jasmin is a good guy its that other guy names organic chemistry that sucks. I'm not gonna lie he has trouble finishing his sentences and has accent so its sometime hard to follow, oh yeah and he gets mad when you ask questions and tells you too look at the stuff he posted on canvas as his answer. Grading isn't too bad since he curves.

his lectures are pretty rough bc he's kinda hard to hear/understand but he's a really nice guy & he isn't one of those professors that tries to make exams impossible. even if the exam was super hard and everyone fails, his curve will save you. theres a reaaallyyy nice curve in the end. (i had a 64% which got curved to a B)
Dr Jasmin is passionate about organic chemistry. He mumbles a little, but he is very clear in his explanations.

Take notes from his lecture slides! Make sure you really understand his slides and if you don't, read the textbook to clarify. Also, go to recitation!! Extremely helpful for practice!! Jasmin can mumble a lot, and he can be very rude so try not to get on his bad side. It became clear who he did and did not like.
He does not care about his students. Terrible at explaining things and if you dont catch on right away, he doesnt help you.

Orgo at TU is a great way to screw yourself. At this point you can take it with anyone because there is an equally likely chance you will get screwed. Jasmin can be pretentious in class to the point where asking questions are not even worth it. He makes up for this by being somewhat approachable in office hours. Average professor.

Orgo is hard first of all, but he makes it fairly easy. Just pay attention in class and do hw problems He is grade If you hate him, i assume u didnt even try and complaining I took him orgo 1 and 2 i struggled in orgo 1, but i read book, solved problems in orgo 2 I am doing good try, if you try, you will do okay

Professor Jasmin is great. His lectures are easy to understand and he posts everything online. His homework is optional but is a great learning tool. He doesn't require attendance however, he randomly will take it and give extra credit. he curves every exam and the final grade.

Dr. Jasmin is the BEST chemistry teacher I have ever had. If you want a good orgo teacher, take him. Yes orgo is hard. No it isn't going to be easy. Jasmin makes it more enjoyable and bearable. He curves the tests if the class average is below a 50% and he curves the final grades. Also, he is hilarious and knows EVERYONE's names. I'm not kidding.
He is a good professor orgo is not easy. He does not require attendance but he knows who comes and who doesn't. He is willing to help you, and giving a lot of additional resources to help.

He mumbles and relies very heavily on powerpoints that just spit the information from the book at you. His exams throw curve balls sometimes, but he's a super nice guy one on one and can be very helpful. I don't love him, but he's honestly probably one of the better orgo professors at Temple.

Sometimes he comes off as condescending or puts you on the spot in recitation but I found that sort of motivated me to try harder in the class. He comes off as a mean guy but he's honestly not, you can tell that he really wants people to do well with all the material and explanations he provides. The tests were curved and pretty easy tbh.

The best professor I've ever had. Really nice guy and very helpful. His exams are tough but they are always curved. Show up to lecture, he randomly gives out extra credit for attendance if he notices people haven't been showing up. He gives you plenty of helpful stuff to study, if you do the work this class is easy.
He mumbles, If you go to his office hours he will help you out, but sometimes he can be mean there. He curves. His quizzes are difficult.

1st- He mumbles words and sentences frequently so TRY to listen to what he says. Give yourself a ;lot of room in notes to draw out mechanisms cause they will need to be memorized and if you take jasmine try to get him for recitation cause he reiterates what we go over in lecture and even goes more in depth and explains it better.

An all-around cool guy who definitely knows his stuff. He tends to rush through lectures and doesn't give much time to write notes, but he posts the PowerPoints online. He also gives plenty of studying tools, so it all comes down to how much you study yourself. If you need help with something, GO TO OFFICE HOURS! He's super helpful then.

I don't care about his accent, his problem is, he hates to teach. He assumes you are a student who does not study. if you go and ask a question, he will say you don't understand because you don't study. I got an A because I studied on my own. If you do problems differently he will mark it wrong, even if the answer is always right.

Pros: He is extremely helpful in office hours and very patient. Final grades are curved. Gives ACS final. Cons: It is extremely difficult to understand what Dr. Jasmin is saying during lecture, he is fine during office hours. Lectures aren't half as helpful as his office hours. Word of advice: Orgo is cumulative, don't keep up &you will stay behind
He is a good teacher, most kids don't want to study but want to have good grades it doesn't work that way all the time.

Not a very good teacher. We were supposed to cover 6 chapters in the course. Jasmine spent ~a month and a half on the first two and the same on the second, then we spent two weeks covering the last two. Very poorly organized, comes to class and just reads off his power point. Doesn't provide a good atmosphere for questions. Do not take.

Great Teacher! I reccomend taking him. Extremely helpful for office hours. Just do about 5% points above the average and you're going to get a good grade. He's so helpful with any kind of question and will go over Orgo 1 stuff if you forget. Such an amazing guy!

Very nice guy, however I would not recommend taking. He teaches directly from a powerpoint and it is hard to understand what he is saying. Rushed through the end of the material. Also, the exams are not extremely difficult if you read the book. Overall, if you want to do well or go to professional school do NOT take.
Teaches out of powerpoints, can't understand what he's saying, exams are difficult. Curves at the end but you still need to do well on the midterms. Lectures are pointless. Nice guy otherwise

Relies too heavily on the PowerPoint, which mainly consists of reactions and figures from the textbook. With something like Orgo 2, you really need to be a lot more active in your approach to teaching the material than just by flipping through slides. The recitation sessions actually ended up being more helpful than the lecture.

Although he is a little hard to understand, he is truly a great teacher if you just ask him for help. He is quick to aid anyone in need and he will walk you through anything you need to know. He was a great relief from Dr. Bloxton. Dr. Jasmin simplified labs and made quizzes easy to understand. He is also a very nice man.

I'm sorry, I can't understand his accent. Even if I could have, I don't know if I would have learned anything in lab. They gave us 50/50 on the last 8 labs because we weren't taught how to do them in the first place. A total mess of a class. Jasmin's not a bad guy, he just can't teach.
very hard to understand what he's saying. keeps everyone in class for a long time. not very good at explaining calculations. he knows what he's doing. not a harsh grader when it comes to lab reports.

I had him for Organic 2 during the summer. Spent time on irrelevant stuff in lecture like nomenclature. Then rushed through the reactions and mechanisms while mumbling. Best thing to do is just read the book and try to understand it as much as possible.

I couldn't understand a word that he was saying but he does know his stuff. For the entire class I did my own thing in class. As he read over and over the powerpoints in the front of the class I sat and read over my own made notes to learn on my own.
not that bad.Very very nice guy,but you'd better have self-study ability and do not leave the class earlier
can't understand a thing he says, completely incoherent. doesn't like when people ask questions, unhelpful. terrible professor. should not be teaching.
Very hard to understand. Mumbles alot and writes very small. He seems like a nice guy but his lectures are basically pointless. Reads directly off of powepoint the entire class period.
probably the worst teacher at temple university, if you don't keep up with the readings in the text book, good luck passing the class because he wont teach you.

He knows his material but mumbles a lot and talks softly which makes it hard to understand what's going on sometimes. Tests are pretty difficult unless you study hard. But curves the final grade for the class so if you really try, you'll pass.

Very disorganized labs. Some TAs are much more helpful but if your TA is not you are very much on your own. The outlines are confusing, multiple documents to follow, prelab quizzes that don't allow you to see where you made mistakes and improve. Why not write a procedure all on one document? It should be quick. Final is difficult too.

She does not clearly say the chemicals you are going to be using during the experiment, but expects you to walk into lab with a chemical reaction scheme. She doesn't take the time to write one clear experiment and will have you read 3 different documents using 3 different chemicals

I understand if your lab wasn't able to get ahold of MULTIPLE chemicals that were in the original experiment, but please need to take the time and write 1 document that has an experiment with clear measurements of those new chemicals. Not having a CLEAR experimental procedure is cruel especially because we need to know the chemical equation.
Worst lab instructor I have ever came across. Students need CLEAR guidelines. Her labs have zero structure. I don't know how she was ever hired for this role.
It's so sad that Temple made her the lab director for Orgo. John Michel is so much better. She should be fired. She cannot conduct a clearly written lab to save her life. The girl is a complete mess.

Very unorganized. Cuts you no slack. Students shouldn't have to read 3 different types of text and lab modifications to do a lab. The class is already hard and she makes everything worse by providing zero structure to do well. Everything good written about her is a lie.

Context, I just came from hs bc of AP chem. This class is very doable but has a weekly pre lab (3+ hrs), lab report (4+ hrs), and post lab quiz (15 min) (aka this is a very demanding class). Professor is very kind and cares about students. Some of the content in this class helped me with understanding the lecture too.
AI-voiced pre-lab videos that sound awful and confusing lab write-ups with disorganized modifications for labs.

Coordinator for chem lab, only complaint is that the instructions weren't very clear. However, Dr Kaur is very understanding and friendly. When my TA called me a failure and told me I can't do anything right, Dr Kaur offered to let me make the lab up with her. She also helped me find out that engineering is what I want to do instead of chemistry.

AMAZING lab instructor- so good at explaining concepts and making sure everyone understands what is happening. Also very nice, and willing to give points back if you deserve it. Honors lab is a lot of work (lab reports take 4+ hours), but she is the best.
Great professor, her lab instructions are clear and not vague at all. TA grading our reports liked taking off random points but she gave them back if we asked. A genuinely nice person too.
Dr. Kaur is amazing! I learned more in lab than in lecture because she explains everything so clearly. Lab reports are a lot of work but she grades easy. Take honors lab with her if you can.

I would def recommend the honors lab over regular. Kaur was very helpful and really helped me understand the experiments well. I learned a lot and it help me understand lecture more properly as well. There were also 30 extra credit points you can get over the sem. However, the reports do take up a lot of time, so be ready to work on them heavily.

Dr. Kaur is one of the best instructors I have ever had, a joy to have instructing me in the lab! I've always loved lab as one of my favorite parts of science education, but Dr. Kaur makes the lab fun with her outgoing personality and ability to connect lecture material to the experiments and reactions performed. Taking her again in the spring!

Dr. Kaur incredible instructor. Her prelab lectures were very understandable and easy to follow. she makes sures that you understand the material by asking you questions and make you think about it. She is an easy grader on the lab reports.

Dr Kaur is incredible. I had her for orgo lab. She gives great prelab lectures that relate what were learning in class to what were doing in lab. Super approachable and cares about your well being. Lucky to have had her as my lab instructor.

the lecture is basically just time to do the worksheets that are due weekly (graded on completion and effort not accuracy) and he guides you through them step by step. the quizzes are based on what was gone over in class and he usually formats it the same way as the worksheets. he is playfully sarcastic and easily sidetracked (less work/easy ec)
He is very sarcastic. He doesn't apologize when he says sorry. He makes us know things.
hes terrible and not understanding of difficult situations or circumstances

He is one of the best professors at Temple. His teaching style is very good and easy to follow. His exams are SO fair and he will not test you on something he hasn't taught in class. No homework and LOTs of extra credit opportunities. Quizzes (~biweekly) make up most of the grade. Highly recommend for Tech Chem.
I struggled through the class but he gave good explanations for stuff. Sometimes things were a bit unclear but he was really good with communicating important updates.

He is just not a good professor. He doesn't explain anything during lectures and just does a bunch of random problems on the board. If you did not take AP chem in high school, then this class is going to be very difficult. Before every exam, I had to teach myself all of the material because he doesn't teach and just assumes you know everything.

TAKEN ONLINE. used one note for lecture ,overall not much different from in person class. does not waste anyones time, material taught is straight forward and can be found in the textbook for more info. Quizzes were difficult. Overall not good teacher

Focus on in class examples over assigned homework for test and you should do fine. All notes are posted online and LOTS of extra credit chances. I found the quizzes very difficult more so than the exams. Quizzes are out of 25 points so not a lot of room for error on problems.

Took Keyer for Tech 1. Really a cool guy, you can ask him any question whenver and he will do his best to give you the best answer. Gives plenty of extra credit and is willing to talk with you if you're having problems. Highly recommend.

He posts all his lectures, and questions on exams are almost the same as what he does in class. He flat out states that he does not hold your hand and expects seniors to try. It's not easy but if you meet with him, he is helpful.

I dont like Keyer, period. He never seemed willing to help and obviously chooses favorites. Course is hard as expected, no complaints there. Lots of papers and work to do, but Keyer makes the class unpleasant.

all right teacher, teaches all necessary content and the course is fairly straightforward. Its demanding as expected for a 5 credit course. The work was fine and manageable. Biggest complaint was him. His attitude towards some students is demeaning. Gave assignments over breaks. Just in general not a good person. Made me dislike course.

I really enjoyed my semester with him. He is nice and willing to help if you ask for it. If you take him for tech 2, the first lab reports you hand in might not be so great but there are rewrites and you'll definitely get a better grade if you edit your work. If you put in the effort you won't fail his class.

Keyers awesome. End of story. he helps his students thoroughly understand the subject. He's great with office hours and he's willing to help his students. He's understanding and will give extra credit assignments when he sees the class struggling. He's definitely awesome and I recommend him to everyone :)
blah blah
He's not bad, but you have to keep up with the reading. And you have to be good at math.

Hes a nice guy. If you actually do the homework (unlike me), or bother studying for the quizzes (unlike me), you will be able to easily get an A (unlike me). Even if you dont know how to do the problems, just write something down and he'll give you some credit. A lot of the stuff in class is review so you'll have a tendency to blow it off, but dont
