The course starts with Ramella moving high-speed and going much faster than any other professor; this results in very basic understandings of fundamental information, so the foundation for general chem is built poorly. However, Ramella is never not in his office, and he tries his hardest to interest the students during long lectures.

A real treat. 4 exams, but they really help you for the final exam. Makes you want to learn organic chemistry to the best of your ability. Best orgo professor hands down... cannot wait to have another semester with him!
Wasn't confident I was going to pass, somehow pulled out a C+, guy does not seem very nice and is difficult to understand in lecture. You will struggle on the tests they are worded very poorly.

I took him for general chem 2015 during my freshman year. It was his first year at temple so since his style of teaching was not good lol it was difficult so his curve at the end of the class was huge. I heard he is still horrible but a nice guy.
AVIOD
Great guy that makes class as fun and interactive as he can for a large lecture hall. Beware of sudden online homework due date changes though. Study for tests

Another semester with my favorite professor! Taking him for both Orgo 1 and 2. If you really want to learn something in chemistry and appreciate it, highly suggest you take him. He really notices how much work each and every one of his students put in. Take advantage of his office hours; he is beyond helpful.

Great professor, my favorite chem professor that I've had in my whole life. I took chem 1 and chem 2 with him and it was great. He is always around to help you, and especially in the flipped class, he helps so much. There is a huge curve at the end of the semester (20 points) so you will pass the class.

Dr. Ramella is brilliant however, he is not a great teacher. He doesn't explain things clearly. His class is difficult but do-able. His curve at the end is magical and will pass you. GO TO RECITATION!!!
If you go to his office hours and make an effort to do well, he will like you and make sure that you pass his class. The course content is hard, and he knows that, so he will work with you.

The love of my life! He admires students who take the time to visit him during office hours, complete the extra credit assignments, and study incessantly. He doesn't focus so much on number values, but rather notices how much time and effort you put into his class. Will be taking him again! Also, attend the PASS sessions... they are SO helpful!

I honestly think Ramella gets a lot of hate for no reason. He's a great guy, very helpful during office hours and not even that bad during lectures. Only skip lecture if you make an effort to watch the lectures online. Exams are difficult but the curve is huge. I had around a 70 avg on exams and ended with an A-. The guy does want you to do well.

Avoid taking him if you can. He's a funny guy and tries to make jokes during lectures, but I have absolutely no clue what he is taking about most of the time. All of his 4 tests are hard and his class make me want to cry. He doesn't require you to get a textbook and gives you a free online one, but you do have to pay for other things.

Dr. Ramella is a great human being, no doubt about it. His lectures are alright and will give you an idea of what you need to study. However, the textbook has rendered me useless since majority of the exam material is actually on a college level and the textbook is not. He is very approachable so take advantage of his office hours!
amazing, a real treat
The best possible way I could spend my Friday's afternoons. Not only the greatest teacher ever, but also the greatest life form ever. I wish he was my father and brother and mentor and god.
Ramella is a great guy but not good at teaching. The way he teaches is just hard to understand overall but he is not out to see you fail. Try in the class and the curve will pass you

Chemistry is hard, duh. Ramella will make you work for it but so will any other science class. WELCOME TO COLLEGE. He is incredibly accessible outside of class, cares deeply about his students and genuinely wants them to do well. Skim the chapter before lectures, show up, practice A LOT, make sure he knows your name. You will do fine. I loved him.

I LOVE THIS MAN!!! maybe he isn't the best "teacher" during the lecture, but he is caring, funny, makes his best to entertain us during the lecture with all the experiment he does. tests are hard, but there's a huge curve in the end. you can always find him in his office. i love how engage he is with his us and how he wants us to LEARN.

Literally can't describe the amount of stress this class caused me. I have never been so lost in a class. Blames the class if the exam average is low. Gives hw before the material is taught. You're teaching yourself everything. Lecture is confusing and he makes you feel like an idiot for asking questions in recitation. Good guy, but can't teach

Sometimes his lectures can be confusing, but he offers an incredible amount of help outside the class and is super helpful in office hours. You DO have to put in a good amount of work, but if you truly strive to learn and do your best he will recognize it. He is super caring and gets to know students on a personal level.

Dr Ramella teaches not so hard topics in hardest way imaginable, I am always lost at his lectures. I do a lot of self teaching and everything is crystal clear, however it is annoying a bit. He is smart and funny, and gives couple extra credits assignments which helped my grade a lot, he also curves. I would never take him again.

Definitely does not give you an easy A, but his class is very curve heavy. He uploads previous tests on the Blackboard page for you to practice from and is always there to help you There is a lot of work, but it is well worth it. You don''t have to attend class because he uploads lectures online. Go to the review sessions before tests, they help!

Professor Ramella is a very smart man but he can not teach and makes his tests VERY hard. He manages to find a way to teach every topic in the most difficult way possible. The final has nothing to do with the material he taught us and everything about this class is hard. Be prepared to teach yourself all of the material.

Greatest professor ever. He makes the test and homework hard because it's basically what you'll be doing in the real world, plus he drops the lowest exam anyways. He curves like super hard, usually 15-20%. Basically if you can get a 75-80 you get an A. However do expect to read the textbook and study like 3-4 hours for this class.

I've never felt so lost about a class in my life. I've had 5 breakdowns because of this single class. If I could do one thing and go back in time it would be to come to this website and look him up because honestly he's one of the worst professors I've ever had and I've never regret taking a class so much before in my existance.

I learned more from the book than his lectures and power points. We usually are given the hardest homework problems where it's hard to even find on the internet and then go over that lesson in class (kind of). Like we are expected to know all the material before we lecture over it. you have got to catch that curve otherwise I would of failed.

Dr. Ramella was easily one of the worst professors I've ever had at Temple. He is a terrible lecturer and he provides students with nothing less than the most complicated way to solve the problems. The class also involves a lot of homework with confusing due dates - I missed a lot of assignments because I wasn't clear what was graded.

Good person. Bad teacher. You are on your own without a doubt. Tough and makes your semester hell but it's likely you'll get through it if you put in a ridiculous amount of effort. He tries to keep the class engaged and awake with jokes and piazza questions. Exam average is always an F, but the curve is everyone's savior.
I got a C in both gen chem 1 and 2. He is really great during office hours but not good at teaching. Take someone else if you want to learn

Would not recommend if you want to learn I felt lost the whole semester and switched my major bc I realized science is not my forte. He is a nice guy and is willing to help in office hours, but the material is just way to hard and you should not be relying on office hours to teach you every lesson. Curve is the only thing that didn't ruin my GPA.

(I took AP Chem in high school). Did average on the tests but the 10-20% curve always pushed me to an A. Avg was around 50% per test. Don't take if you don't know Chem well. Tests are MC/open ended, hard. He reads off power points. Boring. Take price if you want to learn tho he is little more difficult and curve is smaller

A lot of people weren't fans of Dr. Ramella, but I know I was. Sure the tests are hard, but if you read the textbook, actually learn through mastering chemistry, and do review exams, the curve should place you in the A range. Plus, he drops your lowest exam. *If you need help, GO TO HIM! He's super chill.

The best way to earn a good grade with Ramella is to pay attention during lecture and making sure you know the material inside and out. Don't skimp on the masteringchemistry homework, those are dummy points that will beef up your grade. Make sure to go over the powerpoint notes, book, recitation quizzes, and his practice packets for exams

Dr. Ramella does have some hard tests, but he gives huge curves which is okay. If you want to pass in his class, expect to read the textbook and do practice problems otherwise, you'll get a bad grade. He will help with any question you have if you go to him. He is easily accessible and reply's to emails fast. Take him if you actually want to learn.

While his accent is a little heavy, if you go to his office hours he will explain a topic to you up, down, and sideways. I was super frustrated early on but by the end of the semester I really improved. He focuses more on problem-solving than concepts and curves generously. I stopped going to lecture and only visited office hours for help tbh

Really have to try hard in this class. I didn't understand the testing style until halfway through the course. Rather than understanding chemistry terms, make sure you can solve problems. If you can do the problems on his worksheets and the problems at the end of every chapter you will get an A, but you really have to study.

I feel like he's teaching something else besides Chem 1; feels like chem 5 to me. After the second exam, i decided to not even go to lecture and went to price. I mean, with the curve at the end, I ended up with a C+. I wouldn't recommend if you actually want to learn. Theres too much homework and prefecture activities.

Dr. Ramella is a very nice guy, but if you are looking to actually learn and understand the material, you are better off choosing Dr. Price. Dr. Ramella gives big curves, so basically 50-60 on an exam is like a B or A-. Its not that hes a bad professor, he is just really confusing and its hard to understand him sometimes.

It was so hard to understand him. He thinks that you know how to do problems and basically just know chemistry and he will assign you hard practice problems. Mastering chem isnt that hard considering alot of it is on google. His test are VERY hard and doesn't correlate to what he teaches. Choose wisely

He does have a very heavy accent and it hard to understand him. However, he is a funny guy and he shows a lot of experiments to clarify the materials. His test are very hard and there is always a surprise question that very hard. He curves which helps a lot of people. Mastering chemistry homework question are hard. If you need help, go to him.

Well Dr. Ramella has tough tests than other professors. However there is a curve at the end of the semester (but you dont want to rely on curves!). No one knows what to expect on his tests (there is always a surprise). Also really helpful during office hours and a nice person to have a chat with.

has a really heavy accent making him very difficult to understand He expects you to come into the class with a basic knowledge of chemistry so skips over all the basics its very easy to get lost. I had a A in chemistry in high school and failed this class homework is nothing like the examples he does in class and he moves quickly and doesnt go back

Had Professor Ramella for Chem 1031. Show up to Lecture as he does cover the material. Mastering Chem does assign more difficult problems than are shown in class, but the book does help with these problems and the questions are in line with the topics in class and there are only 10 questions. Go to office hours of you need help and he will give it

Ramella is a nice guy and is willing to help you don't get me wrong but he is VERY hard. He'll over explain an equation in lecture and barely explain basic problems. He assumes you've mastered understanding it so he'll give you very challenging practice problems. They aren't even found in the textbook so studying from the textbook is useless.

His tests are extremely difficult. Prepare to read the text book and teach yourself all the concepts. The practice problems in the book are too easy for the test. Know everything inside out or you will fail. Luckily there's a huge curve. Do practice packets. Go to CLASS. Go to office hours. Make sure he knows your name.

His tests are ridiculously hard. I study for hours, I have study groups, I do all his practice packets and I still don't do well. There's a huge curve though, so I have a B despite failing the second exam (49%). He has interesting lectures but he forgets to teach you the basics so you need to study!!
Dr. Ramella gives pretty clear lectures, although sometimes he spends a little too much time explaining the concept. He also expects the students to answer the questions he asks them.

Good professor if you're taking data science. Extremely knowledgeable and caring, the labs are not as bad as people make it out to be if you just read the instructions. He is very willing to help during office hours and loves when you're curios.

Dr. Smith is a very caring and knowledgeable professor. When you need help, he is always willing to help during class and in his office hours. He responds to emails pretty quickly and is super understanding when you have difficulties and will extend deadlines.

Professor Smith wasted lecture time yapping about stuff that was not even relevant to the content. He would do labs towards the end of class and none of the content made sense. He barely explained the code. There were group projects, especially the end of the semester. He had too much energy, especially for an 8AM class. Def do not recommend him!

Generally a very good professor, my main problem was his lack of answering emails. I probably sent 15+ all semester and only got a response to one which was not helpful. Other than that though he is very helpful, always will extend due dates if you just talk to him! Hes very nice and so smart!!

Honestly, I did not learn much from Professor Smith and relied on the TA's often, but he is extremely nice and fun to talk too. Very encouraging and doesn't grade hard. I think if you're taking a class other than Data Science he would be a great professor.

Smith cares about the subject he teaches. He provides a lot of resources, including his own handmade primer for the course in addition to homework solutions. Homework isn't graded heavily, and there hasn't been any quizzes 7 weeks in yet. Each exam gets a 6x4in notecard. Cares about student success and subject, just needs to communicate it better

He's a chill guy during lab period. I'll give him a 5 if he'd been more transparent with our grades. I was left with the final letter grade without knowing the scores for most of my reports and no feedback at all, so I don't know if there's any mistake or he just gave the grade randomly. And most importantly, he can never be reached through email.

If he teaches chem tech lab, take him!!! He is a chill guy and he knows how to deal with technical stuff that may occur in the lab. He takes a while to grade reports but trust me, he is not a harsh grader. You'll get at least a B and above if you know how to write a detailed report. Final was chill for me all multiple choices.

Awful! nice man but awful teacher. Attending lecture is pointless when this man just rambles about what he's going to discuss and once he finally does, he does not explain the material and make it easy to grasp. The curve is not that big. Overall, I would not recommend. Chem 2 is a difficult subject and requires a good teacher !!!

He's a very kind person, yet I wasn't a good student for him. His teaching and lectures were really not meant for me.. maybe it's different for others. I wasn't faring well in the beginning because I believed I could learn sole from him, but then I attended another his lecture and a another lecture, did practice problems, then I gradually improved.

He's a teacher you would need to see if you were confused on one of the chapters. he's a good teacher, but also a tough teacher who's exams are very hard. but he does grade on a curve if everyone did poorly. If you're going to take him, go to class EVERYDAY and take the lecture SERIOUSLY, if you want to pass.

Took him for Chem 1 and Chem 2 and had to retake both. Took Bloxton the second time around and got all A's. Although he is a nice guy, his tests are extremely hard and most of the averages are around 55. Does Not answer emails. Super hard to reach.

Kids would skip his class to go to other teacher's lectures. Unclear and rambled, but a friendly man who would help if you needed it. Everyone talked about how they regretted taking his class and the class averages for tests hovered around a 65. Curved at the end, but I honestly learned nothing.

This man tries really hard to be a good teacher but it just isnt for him. He should really stick to research or something because he wasted a whole semester of mine. His exams are unbelievably hard in comparison to his horribly poor teaching skills. Bloxton who is an amazing professor gives exams that makes sense. Would never take Smith again.

Smith is a good person, just not a good teacher. You could tell that he wanted to teach you, but didn't know how. His knowledge was higher than General Chem, and didn't know how to break things down and teach to students. He would present you with the information and if you asked how to do it, he struggled because it came 2nd nature to him.

Many stupid kids take his classes and he gives a HUGE curve. so basically a C on his exams are an A. Other than that, I dont go to lecture cause its pointless and you need to teach yourself. I have a chem backgroud so its easy but idiots mostly struggle in his class so ride the curve if you're good in chem :)
Failed 35% of my Spring '14 class but I did not think it was that bad. Did the book problems and practice test a week before the exam and got a B.
He's not difficult at all as long as you do masteringchem and try his in-class examples and practice tests. Had 70 m/c questions for the final but was still manageable.

really nice guy but does not know how to teach. Going to lecture doesn't really help. Best way to study for this class would be doing textbook chapter problems. The one good thing is his curves are really big so as long as you beat the class average on his exams you should do ok.

Really nice guy but horrible teacher who doesn't know how to teach. Going to lecture doesn't really help much. Best advice to study for his exams is to do book problems. Gives really big curves so as long as you beat the class average you should do ok in his class.

Hard class, but Smith will meet with you anytime, anywhere to try to help you. Grades is based mostly on doing the homework and the 3x5 notecard allowed for formulas on the test. Test questions from homework. Smith is nice but super-slow in his lectures. Be prepared, this is a quantum physics course, even if not stated.

His lectures are a bit boring but his tests are easy. If you just do the problems in the back of the chapter and outline the chapters you'll get an A. A lot of mastering chem problems but same with all the other professors. This was one of my favorite classes.

This teacher is so busy with his research, that he has no time for his students. If he cannot spend time and effort to prepare his lectures, then my learning will be limited. Very disappointed with my final grade for the class.

I had this guy for Tech 1 and he was SO boring and only taught from the slides. He's a physical chemist so he's a little more interesting in 3105 but not much. He's so smart but he can't explain it so you can understand. He also takes forever to grade and post helpful material so I feel like I'm set up to fail. Expect to learn alot on your own.

Not the most interesting material but he does his best to get through it. Go to class, take notes and do book problems thats it! Ppl that complain on here dont go to class and expect an A. Gotta put in some effort and do book problems bc he does cram alot on exams but they are very manageable as long as you practice. Overall its a pretty easy A
2nd time taking Chem 1032, and his exams are a lot easier than Dr. Price's. However, he's an extremely dry lecturer. It's your choice.

I can't stand being in his class. They make you buy the textbook and the solutions Manuel, but then he doesn't assign problems from the book. He just makes up random ones, which are tweeked differently then the book so you are in the dark constantly. Also he does not teach. You'll sit there for 50 minutes and get nothing from it.

Don't know why there's a lot of hate for this guy. Easy class, go and see him during his office hours and he'll help you out. Read the textbook, do problems in the back, and you'll be on your way to an A.

If you are going to take him, be prepared to teach yourself chem. His class isn't that bad at all if you do study and know the material real well. All exams are multiple choice but don't get so happy.

Such a BAD teacher. He does not know how to teach and does not get any teaching done. He takes the first 30 minutes of class going over what you learned the class before. You will never be prepared for his exams because he doesnt go over most of it he just rambles in class on pointless information. I recommend to NOT take him if you plan to learn.

Professor Smith is a terrible teacher. You can literally not come to class and wouldn't miss anything. He wastes too much time telling you what you should do to prepare and rambling instead of teaching you. Does not provide adequate reviews for tests and most times you will be lost when it comes to lab. Does not allow retests either.

Probably the worst professor I've had thus far here at Temple. MasteringChemistry takes hours to complete and the lectures usually consist of a summary of the little material that was gone over the previous class as well as some new material. Seems like a nice guy, but horrible teacher. I wouldn't recommend him.

I feel as if all the bad comments are coming from Gen Chem 1 students and that is true! I'm an honest person to tell you, even though this guy may seem nice that doesnt make up for who you feel after every exam. Masteringchem is your life. Most of his exams are multiple choice but dont be too happy!!

Just because Dr. Smith has a Ph.D. does not mean he is a good teacher. His lectures have nothing to do with the exams (which are extremely difficult and math-based). He also assigns Mastering Chem. problems every week which are also difficult and time consuming, but they are more like the tests. I highly advise against taking him. Spare yourself.

Tech has the potential to be a hard class; Dr. Smith made it an easy A. Exams were insanely easy compared to book problems. He did his best to cover a boring subject (I love chem, but tech is really not interesting), so I don't know why people complain about his teaching. Very kind and will help if you ask him.

He means well, but truly has no idea how to approach teaching. He will lecture on a single slide for 3 class periods. Also, he summarizes one before beginning the powerpoint and then the slide on the powerpoint. Out of a 50 min class he summarized for 25 on avg. I would say avoid him, but he was easy.

Dr. smith is a nice person, hes there for help if you need him in his office hours. Hes exams are very difficult and have little to do with his lectures. Masteringchemistry is assigned every week and will be your life! But be sure to do them because about 1/3 of the questions appear on the exam which is multiple choice. basically your on you own!

I don't know what the other people below me are saying he is NOT good guy! He tries every possible way to fail you in order to keep his job! Reading the book doesn't help and he can't teach at all! He is nice on the outside but not on the inside. AVOID!

go over the notes, attend every class, do the problem sets and ask questions. Buy the ACS exercise booklet coz u won't get much info about it online like the orgo chem. Smith will help u every time u have questions in his "Office hour". Read thru the book and take key points from it, thats how i got my A-

Terrible professor. He does not tell you straight forward things to focus on for the exam and his questions end up being along the lines of drawing a stupid figure from the book. There is no curve in the class at all. His tests do progressively get easier but if you screw up one test you're ultimately screwed for a decent grade.

He seems like a nice guy. Always came in with a good attitude. Eager to teach. His notes were hard to study from so I stuck with the book. Exams were brutal but there was a big curve. I stayed 10-20 points above the average and finished with a B. Final is ACS, also brutal.
Decent Professor. Hard. I got an A-.

Dr. Smith is a great guy and gives the student a challenge. For all the people out there who talk about about his Gen chem I course, their comments are irrelevant. To get the A, do as follows: read the book, do mastering chemistry, do EVERY problem in the back of the book. Problem solving is ESSENTIAL.

Nothing against Smith personally, I just feel he has chosen the wrong profession. He is an awful teacher and although the class is passable you learn nothing. His average test grades are in the 50's and he blames the students but if everybody is doing that poorly it is clearly a reflection upon the teaching. If you want to learn, don't take him.

Smith is passionate teacher. His TA is helpful also. He wants students to go extra miles. His grading is generous. In order to do good on his exams, the students need to complete his homework sets. seek for his TA?s help on the hard problems.Try to understand the topics as much as possible instead of memorizing the materials mechanically.

The thing is for some people Chemistry isnt easy and for others its a piece of cake. This guy really knows his stuff, but you will have to put your butt down and actually study and read over material to get the grade you want, its not high school kids! Go to his office and he will help you, he is very helpful if you go to his office!And do your HW!

Smith is a very smart individual, but too smart for his own good. You essentially teach yourself. The material covered in lecture is nothing like the material included on tests. The tests are nearly impossible to pass. I would recommend taking someone else.

Nice guy. Definitely smart, but lacks teaching skills. At Temple you're supposed to teach yourself chem, so the lecturer is really more of a motivator than a giver of knowledge. Smith is not a good motivator. You could increase your chances of becoming interested in chem 1 by taking Dr. Price. Smith is inconsistently inconsistent in his approach.

This is essentially a self-taught course. Smith is approachable and friendly, but he only outlines what you need to know, he doesn't teach the content. His exams are needlessly elaborate. Expect to spend four or more hours on each homework assignment. He doesn't teach the fundamentals of chemistry, just problem solving. Nice man, poor teacher.
He's really really really bad. This guy is the worst professor temple has to offer...at least from my experience

His tests are nearly impossible!! The averages are usually around a 50%. Reading the book to study for the test doesn't help at all. He gives a lot of mastering chem problems and they are hard. I recommend not taking him if you can help it.

A nice guy, but his lectures are extremely boring and slides are straight from the book. Going to his lectures are absolutely unnecessary because you really don't learn from them. Basically, you're on your own to learn the material from your book. Test are ridiculous, but you can't expect anything less. And masteringchemistry is IMPOSSIBLE.

Ridiculously hard exams which he does not prepare students for. His lectures are merely the tip of the iceberg as far as material covered on the exams. If you take him, you are on your own to learn the material...good luck!

Dr. Smith is a nice guy but not the best teacher. He covers very little in class, and almost all of my learnig was done by myself. His tests were ridicolous and impossible to do in the time that was alloted for them. The plus is that if you have any questions he'll help you during office hours and it's not too hard to get some points back on tests.
A good teacher and a good person.

Dr. Smith is a very fair professor. Yes, I know his test are hard and lengthly, but he is very clear to what is expected and explains topics well in class. Try your best and good things usally will come your way.

Yeah, the tests are difficult, but chem tests are supposed to be difficult. What really matters is that prof Smith does a good job of explaining the material so that you understand the basis of what's going on, and actually cares that you know the stuff. I've had other chem profs at Temple, and in retrospect he was my favorite.

Did not really explain how to do the problems for the online homework (Mastering Chem). His tests are extremely lengthy and the tests were moderately difficult. MASTERING CHEM IS YOUR LIFE!! KNOW IT WELL!!
Good teacher but tests were a little difficult. I'd take him again.

Dr. Smith is passionate about his subject. Though he's a new to Temple, he clearly is not new to teaching. The lectures are very helpful, and he does not take attendance. The tests are not needlessly difficult (as they are in some other chem classes...). As for the user who complained about a 60 average, um, it's called a bell curve... duh.
He sucks. wastes time in class and puts questions on the test that werent up for review. one test the class avg was a 60. find someone else.
Automatic A, I highly recommend him!

Decent professor, really good about answering questions people ask in class. Most if not all of what he goes over in lecture just outlines what's in the book - skipping lecture is pretty common. The tests are easy (multiple choice). Online homework is more difficult but allows for multiple tries, so you can get over 100% even if you miss questions.
Easy professor. Tests are not too difficult, but his mastering chem hw can be quite challenging. All said, you will not regret taking him at all!!1
Great professor. His lectures are clear and he puts together good power points. Tests and quizzes are fair and you will do fine if you go to lecture.

loved her as a prof. highly recommend going to her office hours. do the extra credit assignments. you can drop one exam including the final. prep for each exam (~50% mcq/50% frq): answer the guiding questions, do the achieve mcq, and do the practice exam. final was ~90% mcq with most pulled from past exams

I took this class because everyone said she is a great lecturer and the tests would be direct reflections of her notes and the study guide. I have found that her exams are difficult in the way that she is trying to trick us. The study guides have only half of what ends up being on the test. The practice tests are a waste. She is also mean.

She was really really great! It was a difficult class but I think if you go to all the lectures and pay attention to the practice problems you can do pretty well. She's always available for office hours and is super nice, knows how to put things in digestible terms, and is very organized.

Dr. Wilent is an excellent professor! Lists exactly what she expects students to know for tests with some extra credit opportunities. Students can drop an exam, meaning students that are happy with their grade don't have to take the final. The course content follows the MCAT topics so def recommended for premeds. Best biochem prof at Temple!!

Professor Wilent is one of the best professors here genuinely. She has a warm and encouraging energy to her. Her exams are fair, and she is very understanding of circumstances for extensions. She offers plenty of oppotunities for extra credit. She also provides back exams and this class is very helpful for the bio/biochem section on the mcat.

Dr. Wilent was a great lecturer. There are a lot of bonuses, and 3 very fair exams + a final. This helped a lot for the b/b section of MCAT. One exam is dropped so I didn't have to take the final. You can find the answer to most things in the textbook, her lectures align well with it. She was very helpful in office hours. Take her if you can!
I really enjoyed this course. The lectures were interesting and the exams were very fair. I am studying for the MCAT and find the information I learned in this class very helpful.
I found her very helpful as my First Year Seminar instructor, I think she's great as an advisor.

I absolutely LOVED Dr. Wilent, I graduated last year but went on to grad school and still Dr. Wilent is my favorite professor I've ever had. She is extremely compassionate and intelligent. I believe this is very well reflected in her course. I was very surprised to see that she had a 3.6/5.0 on here.

She's clear, intelligent, and cares a lot. But, the course content is extremely dense, vocab heavy, and intense to manage. Many people do well in this class and they must be putting dozens of hours in per week to do well on exams because they made me want to cry each time. Coming from someone who got A's in orgo. No curve cuz class avg is high :(

Dr. Wilent is an amazing professor. She does such a good job of teaching and genuinely wanted students to learn and do well. Her tests have a built in curve (out of 25 points but we have 28 questions). Very manageable homework expectations. Tests are not super difficult. Her lecture slides are very clear. I wish I could take her again for a class!

Best STEM professor I've had at Temple. She actually cares about her students and the tests don't consist of things you've never heard of before. It is not the easiest class, but it's because of the difficult course material, not her. It takes a lot of effort to do well on the exams, but as long as you actually put in the work you will be fine!

She teaches really well, but it is hard to understand what information is prioritized for the exam. You'll have to study everything and it gets hard because its a lot of info. It doesn't help how fast she goes through lectures, you'll finish 1 homework and have another due the next few days.

Dr. Wilent is truly one of, if not, the best professor I've ever had. She is great at conveying information in a non-boring, unconventional manner and tries to cater to everyone's learning style. She set up quizzes for each chapter rather than two huge unit tests which made it MARGINALLY easy to do well. She genuinely cares.

I really feel prepared for the MCAT after taking this course. Anyone that is looking to have a strong foundation in the biochem section for the MCAT, I'd def recommend taking Chem 3401. Dr. Wilent's exams do get tricky sometimes & she'll add the most random minute details, but she is extremely helpful. Class averages are always 80-85 so no curve

Applications of Biochem is not an easy class, but if you put in the work you'll do fine. Professor Wilent will oftentimes fly through lectures but she's more than happy to help you out during office hours. The exams can be hard so make sure you understand everything, even details that may seem trivial. Overall, you'll learn a lot in this class.

She's a good professor but she is irritating. the class is full of pre-med students so the averages were so high but if you dont know every single detail you won't get the questions right. be on top of the class or you won't pass. also if you need accommodations dont even bother asking her she won't give u it
Love Dr. Wilent, she is an absolutely wonderful professor. Would totally take her class again.
This was a great class. Dr. Wilent's lectures were very interesting and informative, and she gave a reasonable amount of work.

Dr. Wilent is the perfect example of a professor who cares the material she teaches AND her students. She is fantastic at keeping me engaged in class. She covers a lot very quickly so it can seem overwhelming for exams but she is always happy to answer any questions you have.

Dr. Wilent is probably one of my favorite professors at Temple! I've had her for many classes now, and although the material isn't the easiest, she does an amazing job at explaining it. She is such a kind lady and is always willing to help when a student needs it!!
I just love Dr. Wilent, she is a gem! Had her for biochem and thoroughly enjoyed the class. Would definitely take her again. :)

Dr. Wilent was okay for biochem. She liked flying through topics which were a bit difficult, but also spending a lot of time on others which didn't need them. Going to her office hours was hit and miss: either she is very happy to meet with you or speaks robotically to you until you leave (no in between). Not the worst, but not the best either.

Have had multiple classes with Dr. Wilent. The capstone is designed to help you. She is a generous grader when you put in the work. Most lab reports are in a group and you have plenty of time to do them. One group presentation and several 2 page writing assignments. Interesting class to culminate the biochem program!

Sweetest lady ever! The class was tough because the exams were majority open-ended, followed by an ACS final exam, but definitely do-able, especially if Gen Chem 2 was your forte. Very passionate about the subject she teaches and is super helpful and understanding during office hours if you are struggling. 10/10, would take her again!

Despite not being a biochemistry-oriented class and a notoriously boring subject (sorry to the analytical chem lovers out there), Dr. Wilent teaches it pretty effectively. 7 quizzes (2 dropped), 4 exams, no HW. Extra credit here and there. Textbook not necessary but you'll need to pay close attention to the class notes. Not easy but def doable.

Biochem 1 was a real pleasure with Dr. Wilent! Very clear and articulate lectures; make sure to pay attention to what's emphasized in class. Open-ended exams offer plenty of room for partial credit, but they're not easy! Need a lot of critical thinking and memorization. No quizzes. A few extra credit opportunities were really helpful. Great choice!

When she's happy you would be treated like a gem, but when she's grumpy lmao brace yourself for whatever she throws at you. Be super careful with tests since she will never admit it's her wording and rubric that were wrong. There was one time she was wrong but threatened me that she might take more points off so she didnt have to re-grade my test.

Her test questions are confusing, not because of the problem itself but her wording. Many people already ask what she meant by the questions but she never admits perhaps she's wrong and blames it on the students for not studying hard enough. But the truth is, the materials are super easy yet she made it way harder with her confusing exams.

When she grades cascading problems, she does so unfairly. If you get part A wrong and your answer to part B is dependent on part A, she will deduct full credit. She does so because she grades strictly off of a rubric. She is a good lecturer however and explains concepts clearly. However, when testing it is not always apparent what she is asking.

Great class, a little bit tough to digest all the material and be able to process it and understand it well. However, if you put in the effort and read the powerpoints efficiently, do suggested practice problems, and review sapling, you should be ready for the exams. Plus she is willing to help at office hours. Great teacher and comprehensive class

Dr. Wilent helped make my honors chem lab experience great. I was extremely nervous coming into chem lab since my high school didn't really have chem labs. But she explained everything so thoroughly, and her experience from both academia and industry helped a bunch. She cares about all her students, and she is really helpful and fun to talk to.
Her exams are terrible. The questions are confusing and not clear. You never know what is going to be on it and she does not specify. Her lectures are alright. They can get boring.

Great teacher, true passion for biochem. Must attend lecture to know what to focus on in your studies and write down info not in textbook or slides. Focus studying on what she spends time on in class and you will do fine. Lots to study but what else do you expect.. tests are fair, no curve balls. Very kind when I had questions in office hours.

Dr. Wilent is a really great professor and a very nice person. She's always available at her office, she is very knowledgable and passionate about what she teaches. Her exams are mostly multiple choice which can be a little tough but if you review her notes, read the powerpoints, it's possible to get a good grade. So definitely take her

Says what she expects in lab reportsanalysis but when you write 15 pages of what she wants, its never good enough. She will insult you when you ask questions, even after she says, 'are there any questions'. She speaks robotically towards you and acts like she is non human and has no emotions or feelings. Very egotistical and not down to earth.

She definitely loves biochemistry and interacting with her students. The textbook goes quite in-depth so you'd better attend the lectures and understand how she covers the textbook materials. Exam questions were confusing and difficult but she was a very lenient grader.

She's all right. Her free response questions on the test can be confusing and the multiple choice is hard. Half the class does extremely well and the other half are completely terrible, very little in between.
All I can say she seems like a good person but have a lot of issues inside her biochem mind! Class was boring exams are hard and there is no way out ! She dont care about students at all

One of my least favorite teachers at temple. She always made it clear that you were allowed to ask questions during class but if you did she made you feel dumb. Its almost as if she was insulted that you didn't understand. Tests were impossible to study for. It was unclear what was necessary and what wasn't. Good luck if you take her!
She is good to tell you what to study for the exam if you attend and listen carefully. Sometimes she writes the problems in reverse compared to homework.

Dr. Wilent was a challenging professor, but was clear, organized and very helpful. She not only made the material clear and understandable, but she also would take as much time to help you understand the material during her office hours, and was very approachable and friendly. Easily my favorite Chemistry Professor at Temple.

What do I say Dr. Wilent! Clear, helpful, friendly, caring, and last but not least respect for students. I wish all professors at Temple are like her then my undergrad life would have been so much better. The only regret leaving her class is that I wont be able to have her as a professor next semester.

Dr. Wilent is one the best professors in Temple! She teaches the material clearly so lectures are super helpful. Make sure you do all the problems she tells you to do because her exams are based on those problems and her notes.

AVOID. This person is worse than Stanley and I thought he was terrible. If you're laid back- save yourself the trouble and wait till Smith teaches this. If you like grinding boring, I mean BORING material - go for it buddy. AVOID.

She has an excellent mind. Emphasis on important material. Straight forward, no curve-balls. Reading and understanding of the material is necessary. She loves biochemistry and will pass the love and passion onto you. She has patients and will give you her attention, she is in the moment and knows how to balance her stressful workload. Admirer
Super cool prof!

One of the best professors I've ever had! She's a quirky lady who not only does her best to teach the material but interact with her students as well. She never pulls any curve balls on exams, but they really make you apply concepts. As a biochemistry major, I'll have to take her again, and I'm super excited for it!

One of the best profs at Temple. I've taken her for two Biochem classes and she is a great teacher. Your not going to get an A when you start to study the night before a test. People need to get their sh*t together and stop whining, these are upper level Biochem classes not high school biology. Stop blaming the teacher when its your f.

She is very helpful. Her willingness to answer questions, despite how often you ask, is great. Attending lectures is mandatory (and i encourage it). The lab manual is very clear, making it hard not to get the experiments right. She emphasizes the important topics that should be covered in lab reports. Just show you're making progress through semstr

A prof for the student interested in truly LEARNING and SYNTHESIZING concepts. If you're a whiner who just wants to regurgitate Powerpoint slides and facts on a page you shouldn't be in science anyway. She's difficult but if you pay attention in lecture and study you'll come out with a thorough, deep understanding of the subject. Great professor.

Wilent is the only professor at Temple I have EVER had a problem with. She is inconsistent, confusing, and condescending. I got an A, but she is terrible.

She is very tough. During a lecture she give lots of examples, however her tests are nothing like the problem we did in class. Attendance is not mandatory, but she can give a pop-up quiz. I knew a lot of people were extremely disappointed that took that class with her.

Well... This teacher is awful. Her lectures are nothing like her exams. She enjoys giving 50 multiple choice questions in a 50 minute time frame. She acts like she soooo cool and much better than everyone. Her office hours are "helpful" but in the end, she'll screw you. Temple is a great school and she doesn't deserve to be teaching here.

She is extremely smart and knows her stuff VERY well. She is clear and explains the material thoroughly. However, her tests ARE NOT easy and don't expect to study a little bit and pass. You will need to study REALLY hard and pass. It's not an easy class and she's not an easy teacher. But she's a good educator.

For a 4000 level class she taught as expected. Attend ALL lectures, and take notes on what she SAYS not whats on the powerpoint because you can read that on your own time, reread content in the textbook while going over notes, and most importantly DO THE SUGGESTED PROBLEMS!!! Very helpful and approachable if you have questions, got an A.

These people prior to me are clearly the slackers of the class. They don't understand that this is a 4000 level class and as such require the proper time and effort. I've gotten above a 90 on every exam because I've actually studied. And I've literally studied for each exam for two days before. So please, if you're lazy, shut up and man up.

Her exams are nothing like her lectures. Our averages for all 3 exams where ~58%. Only uses a C curve. Is proud that she confuses the hell out of you. The worst chemistry proffesor i ever had. She is not well prepared for class, always solving problems in class without explaining the concept behind it. Materials are easy but she makes the exams hard and too long. Horrible teacher, avoid her if you can.
She,definitely, should NOT be teaching at a college level! Her teaching is not consistent with her exams.

She's a good lecturer. Her exams are tricky. While you think you are prepared for the exam, she confuses the hell outta you. Exams are not always consistent with her teachings. most of the stuff she didn't teach is in the exam. Very long exams and won't give you enough time to finish it. Backexams does not help.
Very unclear professor! The way she explains the problems in class it just doesn't make any sense. She is just using formulas without any clear and logic explanation. :-(

She should go back to school and learn how to teach!!! You ask her a question and her response is ''You should had learn this in another class!''. I come to school to learn. class is not really hard but it's really impossible to finish the long exam within the time given.

good lecturer. great, concise powerpoints. excellent office hours. willing to help. exams based off of class but a 50 min exam covers a small sample so it's easy to study the wrong stuff. that's part of biochem - learn memorization tricks! she uses C curve. i'd prefer a B- curve. overall, i recommend her and she's nice enough! i give her an A-.

Don't turn in assignments late they are not accepted. I am not sure if it was because she was pregnant with raging hormones, or if she is always that unclear. Def one of the TOP professors at Temple. Outlines all of the material that is covered and has great powerpoints. Sexy as well, very easy class to attend and enjoy.

She's really easy to follow along with in class and makes interesting medical and pharmaceutical connections. All-in-all she's a great professor. We're still mid-semester but started this because she wasn't on ratemyprofessor for some reason.
